one_line_description: All-in-one funnel builder with email marketing, courses, and automations, exposing a public API for contacts and related resources.
auth_summary: Public API authenticates with an API key passed in the X-API-Key header; keys are created self-serve in account settings (max 3 per account, with expiration dates). No OAuth found — the docs even solicit feedback on which auth methods to add next.
access_notes: Fully self-serve: any account (including the free plan) can create a public API key under Settings > Public API keys. No review or approval process.
api_surface_summary: REST API centered on contacts, tags, and contact fields (with cursor pagination and PATCH support); documentation is thin and the developer portal intro documents little beyond contacts/tags — funnels, courses, and payments coverage is unclear.
main_blocker: Narrow, still-evolving API surface (mainly contacts/tags); much of the platform (full funnels, courses, payments) not clearly exposed via API
agent_notes: Surprise: systeme.io ships an official MCP server (contacts, tags, custom fields, newsletters, funnels per their help article) — its MCP appears to cover funnels/newsletters that the public API docs don't clearly document, suggesting docs lag behind capability. I fetched developer.systeme.io/reference/api directly: intro documents only contacts/tags and asks users which auth methods to implement next, so breadth is genuinely unclear. Confidence lowered accordingly.
